title: Women's Political Participation and Intrahousehold Empowerment: Evidence from the Egyptian Arab Spring

data.file: data/raw/358/Data for Womens Political Participation and Intrahousehold Empowerment Evidence from the Egyptian Arab Spring/women_n2008-2014.dta

original_result.source: Table 1, Column 4 (Model 4 — Baseline). ibp composite empowerment index. areg with municipality FE, cluster(ID_2). N=27,782.

notes: TWFE-ONLY replication (only 2 time periods: 2008 and 2014, single treatment event: 2011 Arab Spring). CS-DID, SA, Gardner all inapplicable. Treatment = post_group (pre-computed post x group). group = above-median protest intensity governorate. 22 governorates (excl. 5 border). 272 municipalities. Controls include post x covariate interactions. Conley-type SEs in some specs but Model 4 uses cluster(ID_2). R feols should give coef=4.181, SE~1.053 (small-sample adj difference). | LESSON 7 CASE (2026-04-19, direct-level collapse #3): Paper-auditor EXACT (0.003%). TWFE with 30 ctrls=4.18 (paper Table 1 Col 4); TWFE no ctrls=4.95 (+18%); CS-NT no ctrls=4.59 (+10% vs TWFE with ctrls). Spec A (CS+30 ctrls in 2×2): status=OK but ATT=0.000 NA → Pattern 42 collapse, 3rd instance of direct-level-ctrls-collapse variant (along with 335 Le Moglie 18 ctrls single-cohort and 79 Carpenter 53 ctrls staggered). Consolidates the rule: `direct level ctrls + single/few-period design + doubly-robust propensity score → collapse`. Pentagon of Lesson 7 Spec A behaviors: 25 clean, 79 collapse-staggered, 125 inflate-RCS, 335 collapse-single, 358 collapse-2x2.
